name: Moleta del Terme Trail
name_original: Parque Natural de la Tinença de Benifassà — Running circular por las cumbres
description: This difficult 9.4-mile (15.2 km) jogging route in Parque Natural de la Tinença de Benifassà offers challenging terrain and summit views.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Rossell are primarily found within a landscape characterized by significant elevation changes, with routes ascending over 800 meters. The region features natural parks, such as the Parque Natural de la Tinença de Benifassà, offering varied terrain for runners. Trails often lead through hilly areas and offer views of the surrounding natural environment. Best jogging routes around Rossell The most popular jogging route is Parque Natural de la Tinença de Benifassà — Running circular by the summits, a 9.4 miles (15.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 5 minutes to complete. This difficult route features over 1,700 feet (523 meters) of elevation gain. Another top favourite among local runners is Los Rastells – Selleta de Pallerols loop from la Sénia, a difficult 12.1 miles (19.4 km) path. This route includes substantial climbs, reaching over 2,600 feet (812 meters) of ascent. Local runners also love the View of Tinença de Benifassà loop from Rossell, a 14.5 miles (23.4 km) trail leading through the Tinença de Benifassà area, often completed in about 3 hours 27 minutes. Jogging around Rossell is defined by hilly terrain, natural park landscapes, and routes with significant elevation changes. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging ascents. The routes in Rossell are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from 4 reviews. More than 700 runners have used komoot to explore Rossell's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many jogging routes are available in Rossell?
A: Rossell offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 80 trails to explore. These routes cater to various fitness levels, ranging from moderate paths to more challenging ascents.

Q: Are there any easy running routes in Rossell for beginners?
A: While Rossell is known for its hilly terrain and significant elevation changes, there are a couple of easier routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14888954" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from la Sénia&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option that features less elevation gain compared to many other trails in the region.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging trails in Rossell?
A: Jogging routes in Rossell are primarily characterized by significant elevation changes, with many trails ascending over 800 meters. You can expect hilly areas and varied terrain, often leading through natural parks like the Parque Natural de la Tinença de Benifassà.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Rossell?
A: Yes, Rossell features several excellent circular running routes. Popular options include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e881192300/parque-natural-de-la-tinenca-de-benifassa-running-circular-by-the-summits" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de la Tinença de Benifassà — Running circular by the summits&lt;/a&gt;, which is 9.4 miles long, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38241083" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Tinença de Benifassà loop from Rossell&lt;/a&gt;, a 14.5-mile trail.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or viewpoints along the running routes?
A: The region around Rossell offers several natural attractions. You might encounter the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2479086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de Robert Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or enjoy views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3425404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ulldecona Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The trails often provide scenic vistas of the Tinença de Benifassà area, known for its natural beauty.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by unique natural monuments?
A: Yes, some routes in the Rossell area can lead you near unique natural monuments. For instance, you might find trails that offer access to the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2801736" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portell de l'Infern&lt;/a&gt; or the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3951882" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Millennial Olive Tree of the Four Legs (Canet lo Roig)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging experience in Rossell?
A: The running routes in Rossell are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from 4 reviews. Over 700 runners have used komoot to explore the varied and challenging terrain, often praising the natural park landscapes and the rewarding ascents.

Q: Are there any moderate running trails suitable for those looking for a bit of a challenge without extreme difficulty?
A: Yes, Rossell has 26 moderate running trails. A good example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14888968" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova del Fesol loop from la Sénia&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 7.2 miles long and involves a moderate elevation gain, offering a balanced challenge.

Q: What is the longest running route available in Rossell?
A: One of the longer routes you can tackle is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38241083" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Tinença de Benifassà loop from Rossell&lt;/a&gt;, which spans approximately 14.5 miles (23.4 km) and typically takes around 3 hours and 27 minutes to complete.

Q: Can I find routes that offer significant climbs for advanced runners?
A: Absolutely. Rossell is ideal for advanced runners seeking significant climbs. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14889166" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Rastells – Selleta de Pallerols loop from la Sénia&lt;/a&gt; feature substantial ascents, reaching over 2,600 feet (812 meters) of elevation gain over 12.1 miles.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical points of interest near the running trails?
A: While the focus is on natural landscapes, you might find some cultural points of interest. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5116474" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parish Church of Saints Abdón and Senén (Fredes)&lt;/a&gt; or the charming settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5527451" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vallibona&lt;/a&gt; are located in the broader region and could be explored in conjunction with your running adventures.
tips: From the top, at 947 meters above sea level, Moleta del Terme offers amazing views of the entire Ports valley and Bajo Maestrat. Getting there requires physical effort and a firm step given some technical sectors, but the views will undoubtedly be a great reward!

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this running route?
faqs.answer: This challenging circular route is designed for jogging and typically takes around 2 hours and 5 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and fitness level. It covers a distance of approximately 15.2 kilometers with significant elevation changes.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this running circular by the summits?
faqs.answer: The terrain is rugged and mountainous, characterized by ravines, hills, and diverse paths. You'll encounter established trails, tracks, and even older cobblestone sections. Expect significant elevation changes and uneven, rocky surfaces, so sturdy trail running shoes are highly recommended.

faqs.question: Is this running route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating, significant elevation gain of over 520 meters, and rugged terrain, this route is generally not suitable for beginners. It requires good fitness and experience with trail running.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and wildlife can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience stunning and varied landscapes, including diverse Mediterranean vegetation like wild black pines, beech trees, and extensive boxwood. Keep an eye out for wildlife such as goats, foxes, deer, and various bird species like golden eagles and griffon vultures. From the summits, you'll be rewarded with spectacular views of the mountains and forests.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this running trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed in the Parque Natural de la Tinença de Benifassà. However, it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially given the presence of wildlife and other trail users.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this route?
faqs.answer: The Tinença de Benifassà offers a great climate for outdoor activities. Spring and autumn are often ideal, providing milder temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summers can be hot, so an early start is advisable, while winters can bring colder conditions at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for the Parque Natural de la Tinença de Benifassà?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits required or entrance fees to access the Parque Natural de la Tinença de Benifassà for recreational activities like running. However, always respect park regulations and leave no trace.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this running trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking for this exact route isn't detailed, the park has various access points. Given the route's circular nature and its integration with local paths like 'Camí de les Moletes' and 'Camí de Bel a Rossell', parking would likely be available near one of the villages or established trailheads within the park. It's recommended to check local maps or park information for the most convenient starting point.

faqs.question: Does this route pass any notable landmarks or viewpoints?
faqs.answer: Yes, this running route ascends to summits, offering spectacular views. A key highlight along the way is the Moleta del Terme, which is a significant summit in the area. You'll also experience the general solitude and impressive rock formations characteristic of the Massif del Puerto.

faqs.question: Does this running route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route utilize existing paths within the park. You will find yourself on parts of the 'Camí de les Moletes', 'Camí de Bel a Rossell', and briefly through village sections like 'Plaça de l'Església' and 'Carrer Santa Llúcia'.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a run on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the 'difficult' rating, significant elevation, and rugged terrain, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, appropriate trail running shoes, and layers of clothing suitable for changing mountain weather. A map or GPS device is also highly recommended due to the varied paths.
